title: System and method for monitoring safety and productivity of physical tasks
abstract: A method for monitoring workplace safety and evaluating risks during lifting activities is provided, the method comprising receiving signals from wearable first and second devices, identifying portions of the signals corresponding to lifting activities, excerpting the portions of the signals corresponding to lifting activities, and calculating risk metrics based on measurements extracted from the excerpted portions of the signals, the risk metric indicative of high risk lifting activities.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A computer-based method for identifying risk during physical activities comprising: receiving a first signal from a wearable first device indicative of physical characteristics of the first device over time; receiving a second signal from a wearable second device indicative of physical characteristics of the second device over time; identifying, from the first signal or the second signal, an initiation time for a physical activity performed by a wearer of the first device and the second device; excerpting a first signal segment from the first signal for a time period following the initiation time for the physical activity; excerpting a second signal segment from the second signal for a time period following the initiation time for the physical activity; calculating measurements of the wearer for the time period during the physical activity from the first signal segment and the second signal segment; and calculating a risk metric from a risk model based on the measurements of the wearer for the time period during the physical activity, the risk metric being indicative of high risk physical activity. 2. The method of claim 1 further comprising determining a conclusion time for the physical activity and repeating the method to identify a plurality of physical activities over an evaluation period, wherein the excerpted first signal segment and second signal segment are for the time period following the initiation time and prior to the conclusion time for the physical activity. 3. The method of claim 2 further comprising defining a physical location or particular activity for each physical activity of the evaluation period. 4. The method of claim 3 wherein the physical location or particular activity for each physical activity is determined by correlating each physical activity with a 

 scheduled location or activity for a corresponding wearer of the first device and the second device. 5. The method of claim 3 further comprising evaluating instances of the defined physical location or particular activity and generating a recommendation for reducing the value of the risk metric for future instances of the defined physical location or particular activity. 6. The method of claim 5 wherein the recommendation comprises one of: a recommendation to bring a load closer to the wearer by removing any barriers or obstacles between the wearer and the load; a recommendation to avoid lifts beginning near the floor; a recommendation to avoid lifts over shoulder height; a recommendation to reduce the vertical distance between the origin and the destination of a load; a recommendation to reduce a lifting frequency; a recommendation to allow for longer recovery periods between lifts; or a recommendation that the wearer modify his posture or biomechanics in a specified way. 7. The method of claim 2 further comprising calculating a cumulative risk metric indicative of high risk physical activity over time. 8. The method of claim 7 further comprising outputting feedback indicative of a high risk when the cumulative risk metric is above a threshold level. 9. The method of claim 2 wherein the measurements extracted are: a horizontal location of the first device relative to a second device; a vertical location of the first device relative to a floor; a rotation angle of the second device; a frequency of physical activities during the evaluation period; and a duration of the evaluation period. 10. The method of claim 9 wherein the risk metric defines a maximum weight for a package lifted that should not be exceeded. 11. The method of claim 10 further comprising receiving a predetermined average weight of packages to be lifted during the evaluation period, and wherein the risk metric is used to generate an estimated risk of injury. 12. The method of claim 2 wherein the measurements extracted are: an average twisting velocity of the torso of the user; a maximum moment on the lower back; a maximum bending angle of the lower back relative to gravity; a maximum lateral velocity of the torso; and a frequency of lifting activities among the physical activities during the evaluation period. 13. The method of claim 2 further comprising calculating a productivity metric independent of the risk metric based on a total number of physical activities over the evaluation period and the duration of each lifting activity, and generating recommendations for increasing the value of the productivity metric while reducing the value of the risk metric. 14. The method of claim 1 further comprising extracting data indicative of an angular velocity or acceleration of the first device during the lifting activity and determining an approximation of a weight of an object lifted based at least in part on the angular velocity or acceleration data. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein the metric is an acceptable weight limit for an object lifted, the method further comprising outputting feedback indicative of a high risk lifting action if the approximation of the weight of the object lifted is greater than the acceptable weight limit. 16. The method of claim 2 further comprising triggering an alert to a third party where the risk metric is above a threshold. 17. A system for identifying risk during physical activities comprising: 

 a first wearable device; a second wearable device; a computing device having a memory and a processor in communication with the first wearable device and the second wearable device; wherein the computing device: receives a first signal from the first wearable device indicative of physical characteristics of the first device over time and a second signal from the second wearable device indicative of physical characteristics of the second device over time; wherein the computing device: identifies, from the first signal and the second signal, an initiation time for a physical activity performed by a wearer of the first device and the second device, excerpts a first signal segment from the first signal and a second signal segment from the second signal for a time period following the initiation time for the physical activity, calculates measurements of the wearer during the physical activity from the first signal segment and the second signal segment, and calculates a risk metric from a risk model based on the measurements of the wearer for the time period during the physical activity, the risk metric being indicative of high risk physical activity. 18. The system of claim 17 wherein the first wearable device is a wrist sensor and the second wearable device is a back sensor. 19. A computer-based method for identifying risk during material handling activities comprising: 

 receiving a first signal from a wearable first device indicative of physical characteristics of the first device over time; receiving a second signal from a wearable second device indicative of physical characteristics of the second device over time; calculating measurements of a wearer of the first device and the second device during a material handling activity from the first signal and the second signal; calculating a risk metric from a risk model based on the measurements of the wearer for the time period during the material handling activity, the risk metric being indicative of high risk material handling activity; repeating the method to identify a plurality of material handling motions over an evaluation period; extracting data indicative of an angular velocity or acceleration of the first sensor during the material handling activity; and determining a weight of an object lifted based at least in part on the angular velocity or acceleration data extracted. 20. The method of claim 19 wherein the metric is an acceptable weight limit for an object handled, the method further comprising outputting feedback indicative of a high risk material handling activity if the weight of the object lifted is greater than the acceptable weight limit. 21. The method of claim 1 wherein the physical activity is a material handling activity. 22. The method of claim 21 wherein the material handling activity is one of lifting, pushing, pulling, carrying, or throwing. 23. The system of claim 17 wherein the physical activity is a material handling activity. 24. The system of claim 23 wherein the material handling activity is one of lifting, pushing, pulling, carrying, or throwing. 25. The method of claim 6 further comprising evaluating instances of the risk metric across a group of wearers, and wherein the recommendation is applied to the group of wearers. 26. The method of claim 2 wherein the risk metric is indicative of forces on a segment of the spine, the method further comprising providing feedback where the risk metric is above a defined threshold. 27. The method of claim 2 wherein the risk metric is indicative of a biomechanical posture during the physical activity, the method further comprising providing feedback where the posture detected deviates from a predetermined ideal value for the risk metric. 28. The method of claim 2 wherein the risk metric is indicative of dynamic variables representing position, velocity, and acceleration of the wearable second device, the method further comprising providing feedback when that value is above a defined threshold. 29. The method of claim 20 wherein the material handling activity is one of lifting, pushing, pulling, carrying, or throwing. 30. The method of claim 2 wherein the measurements extracted are kinematic measurements of a wearer's torso, including position, velocity, or acceleration of the torso in one or more of the cardinal planes.
